title: So why do we love Eva again
display_title: So why do we love Eva again
wikitext:
[]: So, why do we love Eva again?             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:33 am Today, a friend asked me why I liked Evangelion so much and I just couldn´t give a serious answer, because... I personally never really thought about it in detail. I guess I just kinda... like it. What about you guys?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:52 am I love it because it dives into the minds of the characters and it's character driven. It's also bold and interesting to watch.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:52 am Rei gets naked often enough it has badass robots

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:54 am Xard wrote: Rei gets naked often enough it has badass robots

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:55 am I like all the long, drawn out surreal parts where nothing happens.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:55 am The question is why you LIKED Evangelion so much, not why you LIKE Evangelion so much, right? For me, it could be called as an important chapter in my life but I am not sure I can say I *LIKE* Evangelion now the same way I would say I like other anime. It is more like something that has become part of my past and what constituted myself, for good and for bad. I never thought I would be shocked so much by an anime, and I never thought I would stop enjoying anime but EoE did it for me (among other things). I guess it was an experience that was so unforgettable that it is no longer a question of like or not like. It is just something that left an indelible impression on you, a lot of them are subliminal beauty but a lot of them are nightmarish trauma. The more you were into anime and breathe and eat anime, the more angst/exhilaration that you would go through. That is Evangelion. Not sure if it helps answer your question or not. But that would be my answer if somebody asked me the question seriously -- if it is just some casual acquaintances asking me I would just shrug my shoulders and say "because the characters are interesting, mecha are cool, monsters/angels are unusual, the pseudo-mysticism are mind-boggling, and the story starts like other anime but ends up in unexpected direction" or something even shorter.

[]: Re: So, why do we love Eva again?             Quote Post by Leighton » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:59 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: Today, a friend asked me why I liked Evangelion so much and I just couldn´t give a serious answer, because... I personally never really thought about it in detail. I guess I just kinda... like it. What about you guys?

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Wed Mar 23, 2011 8:00 am I loves me some biomechanical monstrosities and naked blue-haired clones.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 9:26 am Asuka. ...and compelling characters and genre deconstructions and giant, cool-looking robots and kickin' music and great visuals and stuff. But mostly Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:01 am Because I love the characters, setting, story, philosophy, psychological stuff, angel/ Eva designs, story telling, music and imagery. Why is this such a hard question?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:06 am liquidus118 wrote: Because I love the characters, setting, story, philosophy, psychological stuff, angel/ Eva designs, story telling, music and imagery. Why is this such a hard question for you guys?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:19 am ^ What he said. Also, Misato's/Asuka's/Rei's/Ritsuko's boobs.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:19 am Xard wrote: Because characters are unrealistic, setting is halfassed, story full with holes, philosophy pretentious, psychology debunked Freud lunacy, angel/Eva designs are inferior copies of Ultraman shit, Anno can't tell a story if his life depends on it, music is either plagiarism or crappy, judeochristian imagery is pretentious and meaningless

[]:             Quote Post by Leighton » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:25 am Allemann wrote: ^ What he said. Also, Misato's/Asuka's/Rei's/Ritsuko's boobs.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:34 am MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: I like all the long, drawn out surreal parts where nothing happens.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:50 am I could go on about the various things that everyone else mentions, like character design and plot, but I think we all know what we like about those so I won't bother. Instead, I'll just say that Eva has really impacted me because it was something that I could really connect to. As someone who actually has clinical depression, I was able to relate to a lot of the themes throughout the show, and often identified with Shinji's struggle to find self-worth. Hell, the "congratulations" scene made me cry a bit, which shows just how emotional I felt about the show. I'm willing to disregard all the shows flaws (it has many, like all works of fiction) because it's so powerful and impressionable. Well, for me at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Light » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:55 am Because Asuka. And because I like it, too lazy to get into the details. I'll add that it's the only series I bother to rewatch every now and then.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:56 am Xard wrote: Because characters are unrealistic,

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 11:03 am Bagheera wrote: The rest is a fair cop, but this one isn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Light » Wed Mar 23, 2011 11:04 am Xard wrote: Asuka is overexaggarated ball of ridiculousness and angst adn "supergenius" that never shows

I personally never really thought about it in detail. I guess I just kinda... like it. What about you guys?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:52
